meaning: n.塞口物,随口科白,打诨,箝口物,箝制言论,讨论终结vt.塞物于...的口中,禁止,使窒息,使呕吐,压制言论自由,插科打诨,欺骗vi.窒息,作呕,欺骗,插科打诨
examples: The audience is bounced from gag to gag and conversation to conversation; there is no time for audience reflection.
It was just a gag—we didn't mean to upset anyone.
Each gag was rewarded with a generous belly-laugh.
We wrinkle our noses, gag, perhaps get hungry or sexually aroused, or inhale more deeply.
Apparently Tom Hanks is a genuinely nice guy and went along with the gag.
